title: Education and Literacy
description: RAND research on early childhood, K-12, and higher education covers issues such as assessment and accountability, choice-based and standards-based reform, school leadership, teacher effectiveness, technology, and vocational training. RAND also explores life skills, such as media, health, and financial literacy, as well as how time spent outside the classroom impacts student outcomes.
text:
Next Generation Community Schools in New York City: An Implementation Study of Academic Enhancements in Community Schools
The authors examine the design, implementation, and outcomes of a pilot program that added academic enhancements to 20 newly established community schools in New York City.
Assessing the feasibility of the Count Me In numeracy programme trial
RAND Europe gathered evidence on the Count Me In programme, a numeracy intervention for prisons, to inform a decision on its feasibility and implementation.
Promising Practices for Building Stackable Credential Infrastructure
In this paper, the authors review promising practices for designing and implementing stackable credentials—a short-term credential that builds toward a higher-level credential—to inform the efforts of states and postsecondary institutions.
Analysis of West Virginia’s State School Aid Funding Formula: Recommendations to Enhance Adequacy, Fairness, and Efficiency of State Funding for Education
This report, prepared for the West Virginia House of Delegates, is intended to provide an independent, holistic assessment of the state’s school aid funding formula and to identify opportunities for improvements in the state’s funding strategy.
Mass Shootings in the United States
The authors describe approaches for defining a mass shooting and how these definitions can influence estimates of mass shooting trends. They then summarize findings from the literature regarding the characteristics and consequences of mass shootings.
Data Insights from the RAND Survey Panels
RAND administers several survey panels that are dedicated to collecting and disseminating state-of-the-art survey data. Panel members complete surveys on issues that are critical to policymakers, public officials, and researchers.
Employers as Partners in the Success of Working Adult Learners: The Achieve Your Degree Program
This report provides the first evaluation of the Achieve Your Degree program at Ivy Tech Community College. The authors offer recommendations for implementing employer partnerships and related programming to support working adult learners nationwide.
Building Employer–Community College Partnerships to Increase Working Adult Learner Success
This brief provides an overview of the first evaluation of the impact of the Achieve Your Degree program on working adult learners' credential completion rates and recommendations for building similar employer–community college partnerships.
Assessing the Impact of Grade Retention: A Cautionary Tale of Exclusion Restriction Violations
Reading improvements among flagged students result from added help rather than retention, exposing flaws in RD analyses and emphasizing careful evaluation of policy effects.
Evaluation of Texas's Student Success Acceleration Program 2.0: Interim Report
This report presents an overview of the design of the Student Success Acceleration Program 2.0 and key themes from national and state research on student success, documenting the 33 projects funded across Texas colleges and universities.
